clarity.notes: The artifact remains a clear two-column redline: browser-frame wireframe on the left, annotation/spec panel on the right, and one pin per major editorial region.

hierarchy.notes: The flow now reads as nav/search, theatre lead story, section rail, recent article teasers, and newsletter/footer closure.

typography.notes: System sans and mono labels keep the handoff practical. Uppercase labels have positive tracking and no polished magazine styling was introduced.

spacing.notes: The redline keeps stable region spacing and preserves the desktop canvas on smaller preview widths instead of squeezing the page.

interaction.notes: Pin selection syncs active spec rows, aria-pressed, aria-current, and keyboard arrow/Home/End navigation without adding designer controls.

accessibility.notes: Interactive pins use native buttons, labelled controls, focus-visible outlines, programmatic spec-row focus, reduced-motion handling, and a structured spec list.

brand.notes: Babas & Brasse content is represented through theatre/books/essays/opinion, byline/date/read-time metadata, newsletter capture, submit-writing path, and editorial section labels.

anti_ai_feel.notes: Removed generic feature-card language, replaced the decorative gradient placeholder with plain line geometry, and made the recent section read as article teasers rather than a templated card stack.

state_coverage.notes: The handoff matrix still calls out search loading/no-results and newsletter invalid/success/error states.
